compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
A lean runtime must run workflows without authoring/eval tooling; since extras can only ADD
deps, optionality must be CI-enforced or the base silently grows heavy.

## What
- Wire the extras taxonomy ([agents]; net-new [eval], [tracing]); add a `rebar llm setup` wizard.
- CI: AST import-linter (no heavy top-level imports in core); a clean core-only wheel job; per-
  extra + a union job; guard_import error messages naming the exact extra.

## Scope
- In: extras, wizard, optionality CI.
- Out: tracing backend choice (operator). The [tracing] sink is write-only -- OTel is never read
  back into a rebar decision (oracle-discipline rule, enforced when P3 tracing lands).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Extras taxonomy wired; `rebar llm setup` interactive flow validates with a FakeRunner dry-run.
- [ ] Import-linter + clean-core-wheel CI + per-extra and union jobs.
- [ ] guard_import points users at the exact `pip install nava-rebar[...]` to run.
- [ ] [tracing] documented as a write-only sink (OTel never an input to a rebar decision).
compiled_state.title: WS-J: Dev/prod extras split + setup wizard + optionality CI
